title: taraxacum uliginosum
intro: Taraxacum uliginosum, also known as marsh dandelion or bog dandelion, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant's leaves, roots, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Taraxacum uliginosum include treating digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, as well as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum uliginosum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support liver health and promote overall well-being.
